title: make localunit: record coverage
body:
Inspirsed by CRI-O's coverage logic.  Initial coverage is at 15.7
percent.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *vrothberg* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [vrothberg] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Initial coverage is really low as we test large parts of the code via integration tests.

Shall we enable codecov here as well to display the coverage of the unit tests? Or shall we first work on getting the coverage to a point that we feel comfortable with?
LGTM. I am fine with displaying it here.

@vrothberg needs rebase
LGTM for a start, once rebased, I say get this in then deal with codecov.  I'd a bear of a time getting codecov to accept our data from our repos.

I think coverage for E2E is more valuable, the way things are now.

This LGTM though

> I think coverage for E2E is more valuable, the way things are now.

I looked into it. There are ways to instrument a go binary for coverage but I am not yet sure how far we can get that.

Ready to merge

> I looked into it. There are ways to instrument a go binary for coverage but I am not yet sure how far we can get that.

Currently experimenting with `github.com/confluentinc/bincover` on github.com/vrothberg/vgrep and it's looking quite promising. I guess it would be great to get the coverage from the e2e and maybe even the system tests in case @edsantiago is interested.
